alma
  • Home

Images

Paste or drag images into the conversation. The feature that opened images in a new tab.

How it works

You can paste images from the clipboard or drag them into the composer. The image is sent to the backend as a base64-encoded string. The backend includes it in the Gemini API request. The model processes the image and responds.

Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP. Maximum size: 20MB.

The drag-and-drop problem

The first version only supported clipboard paste. We added drag-and-drop later. The browser's default behavior for dropped files is to open them in a new tab. We needed to prevent that and extract the file from the dataTransfer object instead.

// Prevent default browser behavior
function handleDrop(e) {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();

    const files = e.dataTransfer.files;
    if (files.length > 0) {
        processImage(files[0]);
    }
}

The fix was five lines of code. But we did not find it until we tested on a real browser. The E2E tests do not cover drag-and-drop because Playwright's drag-and-drop API does not trigger the browser's default behavior.

Clipboard paste

Clipboard paste works differently. The browser fires a paste event with the clipboard data. We check for image data in the clipboardData.items array. If found, we extract the file and process it.

The paste handler also needs to prevent the default behavior to avoid pasting the image as text into the textarea.

The macOS app

The Swift app has an attachment button in the composer. It uses NSOpenPanel to select image files. The selected file is sent to the backend as an attachment. The Attachment model stores the file metadata: filename, MIME type, size, checksum, and storage key.

struct Attachment: Codable {
    let id: String
    let filename: String
    let mimeType: String
    let size: Int
    let checksum: String
    let storageKey: String
    let createdAt: String
}

What broke in E2E

  • Image upload button, the file input was hidden behind a styled button. Playwright could not click it directly. We used page.set_input_files() to set the file programmatically.
  • Image preview, the image preview loaded asynchronously. The test tried to assert the preview before it rendered. We added a wait_for_selector for the preview element.

Found something unexpected? Report an issue.
